title: Intermediate Microeconomic Analysis I
description: Consumer behaviour, producer theory, equilibrium, welfare economics, uncertainty, monopoly and oligopoly.

title: Intermediate Macroeconomic Analysis I
description: Income and employment theory, monetary theory, open economy dynamics, economic fluctuations and growth.

title: Principles of Microeconomics with Calculus
description: Introduction to consumer behaviour, producer theory, competitive market equilibrium, welfare economics, monopoly, oligopoly, and strategic interactions, using differential calculus.

title: Introduction to Mathematical Economics
description: Applies calculus to economics, including comparative static analysis and simple dynamic models.

title: Money and Banking
description: Financial markets, Canadian financial system development, financial regulation, monetary control, and central banking theory and history.

title: Introduction to International Trade
description: Trade pattern determinants, trade policy, tariff and non-tariff barriers, protectionism, trade disputes, and liberalization effects.

title: Introduction to International Finance
description: Exchange rate regimes, international financial organizations, monetary policy interactions, and financial crises.

title: Economic Analysis of Law
description: Applies economics to market failure, property rights, accident law, contract law, and law enforcement.

title: Benefit-Cost Analysis and the Economics of Project Evaluation
description: Techniques and problems in benefit-cost analysis of public projects, with alternative approaches and case studies.

title: Introduction to Causal Methods
description: Emphasizes causality, causal modeling, empirical analysis, and theoretical models for policy evaluation.

title: Optimization and Economic Theory
description: Introduces static and dynamic optimization methods with economic applications.

title: Introduction to Game Theory and Applications
description: Explores rational behaviour in strategic situations, with applications across economics, business, politics, law, and biology.

title: Advanced Econometrics
description: Advanced econometric models including nonlinear models, instrumental variables, discrete-choice models, panel regression, and machine learning methods.

title: Econometric Analysis
description: Nonlinear estimation, distributed lag models, time-series analysis, and Bayesian statistics.

title: Monetary Theory
description: Analyzes money's economic roles, demand models, inflation, seignorage, and macroeconomic stabilization policy.

title: International Macroeconomics and Finance
description: Balance of payments, foreign exchange markets, internal versus external stability, and contemporary issues.

title: Economics of Climate Change
description: Investigates climate impact through economic analysis, the social cost of carbon, mitigation and adaptation policies, and climate justice.

title: Principles of Microeconomics
description: Introduction to consumer behaviour, producer theory, competitive market equilibrium, welfare economics, monopoly, oligopoly, and strategic interactions.

title: Principles of Macroeconomics
description: Theory and Canadian policy regarding growth, business cycles, employment, national income accounting, interest rates, exchange rates, money, banking, and trade.

title: Differential Calculus with Applications
description: Derivatives of elementary functions. Applications and modelling: graphing, optimization.

title: Integral Calculus with Applications
description: The definite integral, integration techniques, applications, modelling, infinite series.

title: Calculus III
description: Analytic geometry in 2 and 3 dimensions, partial and directional derivatives, chain rule, maxima and minima, second derivative test, Lagrange multipliers, multiple integrals with applications.

title: Matrix Algebra
description: Systems of linear equations, operations on matrices, determinants, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, diagonalization of symmetric matrices.

title: Elementary Statistics for Applications
description: Classical, nonparametric, and robust inferences about means, variances, and analysis of variance, using computers. Emphasis on problem formulation, assumptions, and interpretation.

title: Introduction to Probability
description: Basic notions of probability, random variables, expectation and conditional expectation, limit theorems.

title: Finding Relationships in Data
description: Modelling a response (output) variable as a function of several explanatory (input) variables: multiple regression for a continuous response, logistic regression for a binary response, and log-linear models for count data. Finding low-dimensional structure: principal components analysis. Cluster analysis.
